summary: DHH highlights a series of customer case studies that Jason (Fried) has been conducting with Basecamp users. He spotlights the story of Marie Thacker from Donordigital, a non-profit that became so dependent on Basecamp they nicknamed it 'BaseCrack.' The post celebrates how real customer feedback validates the product's impact on workflows and organization. It's a brief, enthusiastic showcase of early Basecamp traction and customer love.
key_points: Basecamp published public case studies from customer interviews to demonstrate real-world usage
Donordigital, a non-profit, became deeply reliant on Basecamp after six months, calling it 'BaseCrack'
Hearing how your software changes people's work is described as energizing and motivating for the makers
money_quotes: It's really energizing and motivating to hear about the effects that your software is having on people.
key_insight: Real customer stories showing genuine dependency on your product are the most energizing validation a software maker can get.
